with 30 s skew.\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs();\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\n    // Expired: exp + skew < now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) < now {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\n    }\n    // Not yet valid: nbf > now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\n    if claims.nbf > now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\n    }\n\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\n        .jti\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(&jti_uuid) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n    }\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        if storage\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\n        {\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\n            drop(storage);\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\n    }\n\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\n    let user_id = UserId::new(&claims.sub);\n    let user = {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n    };\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\n    }\n\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\n    }\n\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\n    let granted_db = claims\n        .grants\n        .find_database(database)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\n\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\n        Op::Read\n    } else {\n        Op::Write\n    };\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\n    }\n\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\n        user_id,\n        tenant_id,\n        grants: claims.grants,\n    })\n}\n\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Extract the `Bearer <token>` value from the `Authorization` header.\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Result<&str, AuthError> {\n    let auth_header = headers\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n\n    let auth_str = auth_header\n        .to_str()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n\n    auth_str\n        .strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n}\n\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\n///\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)> {\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\n        (Some(\"tenants\"), Some(tenant), Some(\"databases\"), Some(rest))\n            if !tenant.is_empty() && !rest.is_empty() =>\n        {\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\n            if database.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some((tenant, database))\n            }\n        }\n        _ => None,\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\n\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\n    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n        let body = json!({\n            \"error\": {\n                \"code\": self.0.error_code(),\n                \"message\": self.0.to_string()\n            }\n        });\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n//!\n//! # Authentication\n//!\n//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n//!\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n//!\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n//!\n//! # Request flow\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! TCP accept\n//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\n//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\n//!             └─ route handler\n//!                  ├─ extract Extension<Identity>\n//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n//!                  └─ handler logic\n//! ```\n//!\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\npub mod actor_scope;\npub mod auth;\nmod collection_listing;\npub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\npub mod control_plane;\npub mod control_plane_routes;\npub mod cors_config;\npub mod user_roles;\npub mod gateway;\npub mod idempotency;\npub(crate) mod mcp_http;\npub mod mcp_stdio;\npub mod rate_limit;\npub mod schema_registry;\npub mod serve;\npub mod service;\npub mod tenant_router;\n\npub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\npub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\npub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":43,"oldLines":6,"newStart":43,"newLines":7,"lines":[" //! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`]."," pub mod actor_scope;"," pub mod auth;","+pub mod auth_pipeline;"," mod collection_listing;"," pub(crate) mod embedded_ui;"," pub mod control_plane;"]},{"oldStart":60,"oldLines":5,"newStart":61,"newLines":6,"lines":[" pub mod tenant_router;"," "," pub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};","+pub use auth_pipeline::{InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};"," pub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;"," pub use service::AxonServiceImpl;"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n       sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(),   ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n ops_matrix ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn ops_matrix() {\n    use http::Method;\n\n    struct Case {\n        method: Method,\n        ops: Vec<Op>,\n        expected_status: u16,\n    }\n    let cases = vec![\n        // GET (Read) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        // Write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n    ];\n\n    // Use a route that accepts all HTTP methods.\n    let handler = || async { StatusCode::OK };\n\n    for case in cases {\n        let issuer_inner = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n        let mut claims = valid_claims();\n        claims.grants = Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: case.ops,\n            }],\n        };\n        let token = issuer_inner.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n        let state = make_state();\n        // Router that handles any method on TEST_PATH.\n        let app = Router::new()\n            .route(\n                TEST_PATH,\n                axum::routing::any(handler),\n            )\n            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n                state,\n                jwt_verify_layer,\n            ));\n\n        let req = Request::builder()\n            .method(case.method.clone())\n            .uri(TEST_PATH)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n            .body(Body::empty())\n            .unwrap();\n        let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            resp.status().as_u16(),\n            case.expected_status,\n            \"method={} expected {}\",\n            case.method,\n            case.expected_status\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ── error_code / status_code consistency ──────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn auth_error_status_and_code_coverage() {\n    // Verify every variant maps to the correct status and code string.\n    let cases: &[(AuthError, u16, &str)] = &[\n        (AuthError::Unauthenticated, 401, \"unauthenticated\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialMalformed, 401, \"credential_malformed\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialInvalid, 401, \"credential_invalid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialExpired, 401, \"credential_expired\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid, 401, \"credential_not_yet_valid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialRevoked, 401, \"credential_revoked\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer, 401, \"credential_foreign_issuer\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant, 403, \"credential_wrong_tenant\"),\n        (AuthError::UserSuspended, 401, \"user_suspended\"),\n        (AuthError::NotATenantMember, 403, \"not_a_tenant_member\"),\n        (AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted, 403, \"database_not_granted\"),\n        (AuthError::OpNotGranted, 403, \"op_not_granted\"),\n        (AuthError::GrantsExceedIssuerRole, 401, \"grants_exceed_issuer_role\"),\n        (AuthError::GrantsMalformed, 401, \"grants_malformed\"),\n    ];\n    for (err, expected_status, expected_code) in cases {\n        assert_eq!(\n            err.status_code(),\n            *expected_status,\n            \"status mismatch for {:?}\",\n            err\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            err.error_code(),\n            *expected_code,\n            \"code mismatch for {:?}\",\n            err\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ── revocation via storage fallback ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn revocation_via_storage_fallback() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    // Build storage with the JTI revoked at the storage level.\n    let mut storage = make_storage();\n    storage.revoke_jti(jti_uuid);\n\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    });\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BcRLMXUs4TZUS5Hhv4m4p8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01QqPQtyiL6uPht7wAo243yE","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs","content":"//! Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":575,"cache_read_input_tokens":132304,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":575},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"cc1c03fa-01b8-4915-97d1-edc4d799b3f6"}

SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n7\t//!\n8\t//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n9\t//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n10\t\n11\tuse axum::body::Body;\n12\tuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\n13\tuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\n14\tuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n15\t\n16\t/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n17\t///\n18\t/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n19\t/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n20\t#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n21\t#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\n22\tstruct UiAssets;\n23\t\n24\t/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n25\t///\n26\t/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n27\t/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n28\t/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\n29\tpub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n30\t    let raw = uri.path();\n31\t    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n32\t    let asset_path = raw\n33\t        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n34\t        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n35\t        .unwrap_or(raw);\n36\t    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n37\t        \"index.html\"\n38\t    } else {\n39\t        asset_path\n40\t    };\n41\t\n42\t    serve_asset(asset_path)\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\tfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n46\t    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n47\t        Some(content) => {\n48\t            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n49\t            Response::builder()\n50\t                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n51\t                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n52\t                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n53\t        }\n54\t        None => {\n55\t            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n56\t            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n57\t                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n58\t                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n59\t                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n60\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n61\t                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n62\t            }\n63\t        }\n64\t    }\n65\t}\n66\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"639a3f3a-e27d-4d23-b9e3-a0b9bc507e2b","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:45:23.541Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/src/embedded_ui.rs","content":"//! Embedded admin UI — assets from `ui/build` compiled into the binary.\n//!\n//! The `UiAssets` struct embeds every file under `ui/build` at compile time.\n//! The axum handler serves them at `/ui/*path` with correct `Content-Type`\n//! headers, and falls back to `index.html` for any unrecognised path so that\n//! SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n//!\n//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n\n/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n///\n/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\nstruct UiAssets;\n\n/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n///\n/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}

 .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

data.text: I’ve got one likely-behavioral regression and one clearly unrelated package failure. Before I decide whether this bead can still be closed green, I’m checking the `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` seal path itself and the integration harness land sync, because those are the two levers that would let me fix the package without scope creep.

I read the wrong slice on the first pass. I’m pulling the actual `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` body and the `Land()` sync path next so I can tell whether the concurrent test failure is a checkout-sync issue or a genuine checkpoint-policy regression.

I still need to rule out one more possibility: the concurrent integration test may be dirtying a DDx bookkeeping file I haven’t allowed yet, such as the bead archive, rather than failing because of stale checkout content. I’m searching the store/land paths for those extra tracker files before I touch the policy again.
I tripped over the worktree path again on that read. Retrying with the exact directory so I can see whether the store layer touches any archive or run-state files that still need to be allowed.

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev -x \\\"$AXON_BIN\\\" ]]; then\\n+    fail \\\"Binary not found at $AXON_BIN after build\\\"\\n+fi\\n+\\n+# ── Start server ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n+info \\\"Starting axon-server on HTTP :${AXON_HTTP_PORT} gRPC :${AXON_GRPC_PORT}…\\\"\\n+AXON_JWT_KEY=\\\"${AXON_JWT_KEY}\\\" \\\\\\n+AXON_NO_AUTH=true \\\\\\n+AXON_HTTP_PORT=\\\"${AXON_HTTP_PORT}\\\" \\\\\\n+AXON_GRPC_PORT=\\\"${AXON_GRPC_PORT}\\\" \\\\\\n+AXON_SQLITE_PATH=\\\":memory:\\\" \\\\\\n+\\\"${AXON_BIN}\\\" serve --storage memory \u0026\\n+SERVER_PID=$!\\n+\\n+cleanup() {\\n+    kill \\\"${SERVER_PID}\\\" 2\u003e/dev/null || true\\n+    wait \\\"${SERVER_PID}\\\" 2\u003e/dev/null || true\\n+}\\n+trap cleanup EXIT\\n+\\n+# Wait for the server to be ready.\\n+for i in $(seq 1 20); do\\n+    if curl -sf \\\"${BASE_URL}/health\\\" \u003e /dev/null 2\u003e\u00261; then\\n+        info \\\"Server ready after ${i} attempts\\\"\\n+        break\\n+    fi\\n+    sleep 0.3\\n+    if [[ $i -eq 20 ]]; then\\n+        fail \\\"Server did not become ready in time\\\"\\n+    fi\\n+done\\n+\\n+# ── Mint a JWT ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n+# We need to produce a HS256 JWT. JWT_AVAILABLE=false\\n+else\\n+    JWT_AVAILABLE=true\\n+    NOW=$(date +%s)\\n+    EXP=$((NOW + 3600))\\n+\\n+    mint_token() {\\n+        local grants_json=\\\"$1\\\"\\n+        python3 - test `cutover_no_auth_header_falls_through_to_no_auth_201` confirms |\\n\\n### Summary\\n\\nThe HTTP data-plane side is well-implemented: the optional JWT layer, audit attribution threading via thread-local, and the comprehensive test suite are solid. However, AC #5 (gRPC JWT verify path in `check_auth`) is completely absent from the diff — the gRPC service received only the header rename, not the JWT authentication path.\\n\\n### Findings\\n\\n- **BLOCK — gRPC JWT verify path missing**: `crates/axon-server/src/service.rs` `check_auth` was not modified to accept JWT credentials via the `authorization` metadata key. The bead description explicitly requires \\\"Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\\\" and \\\"Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\\\" No JWT-related imports, no `ResolvedIdentity`, no `JwtIssuer` reference exists in the gRPC service diff.\\n- **REQUEST_CHANGES — Thread-local attribution pattern is fragile**: `handler.rs` uses a `thread_local!` + `RefCell` to pass attribution from gateway to handler. If the harness already intended the checkout to stay current, this just makes that intent explicit.

47:1203093 1114808       24:16  6.3  0.1 /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -C /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-796cb50a-20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9 -m gpt-5.4-mini <execute-bead>   <instructions> You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-796cb50a">     <title>execute-bead: prevent checkpoints from absorbing implementation changes</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Live `ddx work` attempts are landing substantial implementation changes inside `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead ...` commits instead of the required conventional substantive commit tagged with `[ddx-<id>]`. This weakens the execute-bead audit trail: cost, latency, retry, and review tooling can no longer reliably treat the checkpoint as tracker/caller-dirt bookkeeping and the tagged commit as the bead implementation.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:542-599` defines `checkpointPreDispatchDirt`, which stages `git add -A -- .` through a temporary index and creates a `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead <attempt>` commit.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1050-1064` runs `commitTrackerLocked` and then `checkpointPreDispatchDirt` before resolving the base revision and creating the isolated execute-bead worktree.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:17-29` and `:124-138` assert the lock-scope and HEAD-race behavior, but not the content contract for checkpoint commits.
- Observed evidence: `df5b591ec chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260513T175830-e116edd8` contains 305 lines of readiness-timeout implementation across seven code/test files, and `c53f2b0fe chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260513T183205-80b61705` contains 170 lines of `readiness_checks` implementation across three files. The later tagged commit for `ddx-90b84d73` contained only follow-on CLI/docs plumbing, so the main implementation was attributed to the checkpoint.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a checkpoint content guard so pre-dispatch checkpoint commits cannot silently absorb ordinary implementation files. The guard should either: (a) restrict checkpoints to known durable tracker/evidence/caller-dirt paths and reject code/test paths with an actionable execution failure, or (b) require dirty parent checkout implementation files to be committed under the active bead's `[ddx-<id>]` commit before checkpointing. Preserve the existing serialized lock behavior that prevents concurrent HEAD CAS failures.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove the pre-dispatch checkpoint mechanism entirely. Do not weaken the existing concurrent worker lock tests. Do not rewrite existing history to repair already-landed checkpoint commits.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtRejectsImplementationPaths` or equivalent proves dirty `cli/internal/...` code/test files in the parent checkout are not committed as `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead ...`.
2. `TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtAllowsTrackerAndEvidencePaths` or equivalent proves legitimate tracker/evidence bookkeeping still checkpoints when needed.
3. `TestExecuteBeadCheckpointDoesNotAbsorbSubstantiveWork` or equivalent exercises `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` and verifies the checkpoint commit contains only allowed paths while the bead implementation remains in a `[ddx-<id>]` substantive commit or the attempt fails with actionable evidence.
4. Existing concurrency regressions including `TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace` and `TestRun_LockScopeIncludesSynthesizeCommit` remain green.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirt|TestExecuteBeadCheckpointDoesNotAbsorbSubstantiveWork|TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace|TestRun_LockScopeIncludesSynthesizeCommit" -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:agent, area:git, kind:bug, phase:build, reliability, audit</labels>     <metadata spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="c66cf4aaf22692f23df9c7112fbf0cc29610bfcc" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-796cb50a">     <title>execute-bead: prevent checkpoints from absorbing implementation changes</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Live `ddx work` attempts are landing substantial implementation changes inside `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead ...` commits instead of the required conventional substantive commit tagged with `[ddx-<id>]`. This weakens the execute-bead audit trail: cost, latency, retry, and review tooling can no longer reliably treat the checkpoint as tracker/caller-dirt bookkeeping and the tagged commit as the bead implementation.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:542-599` defines `checkpointPreDispatchDirt`, which stages `git add -A -- .` through a temporary index and creates a `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead <attempt>` commit.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1050-1064` runs `commitTrackerLocked` and then `checkpointPreDispatchDirt` before resolving the base revision and creating the isolated execute-bead worktree.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go:17-29` and `:124-138` assert the lock-scope and HEAD-race behavior, but not the content contract for checkpoint commits.
- Observed evidence: `df5b591ec chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260513T175830-e116edd8` contains 305 lines of readiness-timeout implementation across seven code/test files, and `c53f2b0fe chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260513T183205-80b61705` contains 170 lines of `readiness_checks` implementation across three files. The later tagged commit for `ddx-90b84d73` contained only follow-on CLI/docs plumbing, so the main implementation was attributed to the checkpoint.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a checkpoint content guard so pre-dispatch checkpoint commits cannot silently absorb ordinary implementation files. The guard should either: (a) restrict checkpoints to known durable tracker/evidence/caller-dirt paths and reject code/test paths with an actionable execution failure, or (b) require dirty parent checkout implementation files to be committed under the active bead's `[ddx-<id>]` commit before checkpointing. Preserve the existing serialized lock behavior that prevents concurrent HEAD CAS failures.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove the pre-dispatch checkpoint mechanism entirely. Do not weaken the existing concurrent worker lock tests. Do not rewrite existing history to repair already-landed checkpoint commits.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtRejectsImplementationPaths` or equivalent proves dirty `cli/internal/...` code/test files in the parent checkout are not committed as `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead ...`.
2. `TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirtAllowsTrackerAndEvidencePaths` or equivalent proves legitimate tracker/evidence bookkeeping still checkpoints when needed.
3. `TestExecuteBeadCheckpointDoesNotAbsorbSubstantiveWork` or equivalent exercises `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` and verifies the checkpoint commit contains only allowed paths while the bead implementation remains in a `[ddx-<id>]` substantive commit or the attempt fails with actionable evidence.
4. Existing concurrency regressions including `TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace` and `TestRun_LockScopeIncludesSynthesizeCommit` remain green.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCheckpointPreDispatchDirt|TestExecuteBeadCheckpointDoesNotAbsorbSubstantiveWork|TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace|TestRun_LockScopeIncludesSynthesizeCommit" -count=1` passes.

Blocked on the repo-wide pre-commit gate, so I did not commit.

What changed:
- Added a checkpoint content guard in [execute_bead.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-796cb50a-20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go) that:
  - allows DDx bookkeeping paths,
  - rejects ordinary implementation paths with an actionable error,
  - ignores internal lock/temp noise, and
  - keeps the existing lock-serialized pre-dispatch flow.
- Reworked the checkpoint tests in [execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-796cb50a-20260513e185530-1bc8b7f9/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go) to cover allow/reject behavior and the `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` failure path.
- Updated the concurrency regression seed in [execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-796cb50a-20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_concurrent_predispatch_test.go) to use allowed bookkeeping dirt.
- Adjusted the integration harness in [integration_helper_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-796cb50a-20260513T185530-1bc8b7f9/cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go) so landed worktrees are synced before the next bead.
